payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

move.claim: â infinitely many Q with pairwise coprime m_1,...,m_k, â m_i = Q, residues a_i s.t. âª_i (a_i + m_i â¤) covers [1, H] with H > c \frac{\log \log Q \log \log \log \log Q}{(\log \log \log Q)^2} \log Q for any c>0.
move.rationale: CRT covering systems from ErdÅs-Rankin method, optimized for iterated log growth using prime powers or novel moduli sets.
move.test_plan: Construct explicit small covering (e.g., first primes for Hâ¼ log Q log log Q), scale to match bound; check density â 1/m_i <1.
dependencies: Chinese Remainder Theorem
Dirichlet primes in AP
move.expected_effect: Yields prime-free intervals around multiples of Q via sieve.
move.risk: high (requires specific covering construction for this exact growth)
move.why_this_applies_here: Euclid construction transfers: product+1 sieves interval like moduli covering residues; adapts to larger gaps via more moduli.
move.how_to_falsify_fast: For Q= primorial #30 â¼10^12, compute max H coverable; if H < bound for c=1, falsify.

payload.query: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\] formalize: â infinitely many Q with pairwise coprime m_1,...,m_k, â m_i = Q, residues a_i s.t. âª_i (a_i + m_i â¤) covers [1, H] with H > c \frac{\log \log Q \log \log \log \log Q}{(\log \log \log Q)^2} \log Q for any c>0.

move.claim: For any C>0, the existence of infinitely many N such that (N, N+H] is prime-free with H > 2C \frac{\log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log \log N)^2} \log N implies infinitely many n with p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2} \log n, where n = \pi(N).
move.rationale: Standard transfer: p_{\pi(N)+1} > N + H and p_{\pi(N)} \le N imply gap > H. By PNT lower bound \pi(N) > N / (2 \log N), \log n > \log N - \log\log N - O(1); iterated logs converge sufficiently fast that the functional form transfers with factor 2 for large N.
move.test_plan: Symbolically verify using \pi(N) > c N / \log N (c=1/2) that f(n) \sim f(N) with f(t) = \frac{\log\log t \log\log\log\log t}{(\log\log \log t)^2} \log t; compute lim \frac{f(\pi(N))}{f(N)} = 1.
dependencies: Prime Number Theorem: \pi(N) > N / (2 \log N) for large N
move.expected_effect: Establishes equivalence up to constants, completing Milestone 1.
move.why_this_applies_here: Direct analog to interval-to-max-gap reductions in Erd\H{o}s-Rankin proofs; avoids prior timeout by quantifying transfer factor.
required_preconditions: PNT lower bound formalized; iterated logs defined for N > exp(exp(exp(e^e)))
move.how_to_falsify_fast: Numerically check for N=10^{100}: compute n=\pi(N) approx, ratio f(n)/f(N) <1/2?

move.claim: \exists infinitely many Q>1 with pairwise coprime m_1,\dots,m_k \ge 2, \prod m_i = Q, residues a_i \pmod{m_i} s.t. \bigcup_i (a_i + m_i \mathbb{Z}) \supseteq [1,H] where H > c \frac{\log \log Q \log \log \log \log Q}{(\log \log \log Q)^2} \log Q for any c>0.
move.rationale: Core combinatorial object for sieve constructions; pairwise coprimality enables CRT solvability for prime-free intervals around multiples of Q.
move.test_plan: Construct greedily: select distinct primes as m_i, choose a_i to cover maximal new residues in [1,H]; iterate until prod=Q exceeds threshold, check H bound.
move.expected_effect: Provides CRT covering lemma, enabling Milestone 2.
move.why_this_applies_here: Precisely matches the formalized subgoal; preconditions uncertain (construction details), but standard in large gap proofs.
required_preconditions: Greedy covering achieves logarithmic density; pairwise coprime primes suffice
move.how_to_falsify_fast: For small Q=prod first k primes, compute minimal uncovered residues in [1, log Q * poly loglog Q]; if > bound, falsify.

moves.rationale: Standard transfer: p_{\pi(N)+1} > N + H and p_{\pi(N)} \le N imply gap > H. By PNT lower bound \pi(N) > N / (2 \log N), \log n > \log N - \log\log N - O(1); iterated logs converge sufficiently fast that the functional form transfers with factor 2 for large N.
moves.test_plan: Symbolically verify using \pi(N) > c N / \log N (c=1/2) that f(n) \sim f(N) with f(t) = \frac{\log\log t \log\log\log\log t}{(\log\log \log t)^2} \log t; compute lim \frac{f(\pi(N))}{f(N)} = 1.
dependencies: Prime Number Theorem: \pi(N) > N / (2 \log N) for large N
moves.expected_effect: Establishes equivalence up to constants, completing Milestone 1.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Direct analog to interval-to-max-gap reductions in Erd\H{o}s-Rankin proofs; avoids prior timeout by quantifying transfer factor.
required_preconditions: PNT lower bound formalized; iterated logs defined for N > exp(exp(exp(e^e)))
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: Numerically check for N=10^{100}: compute n=\pi(N) approx, ratio f(n)/f(N) <1/2?

moves.claim: \exists infinitely many Q with pairwise coprime m_1, \dots, m_k, \prod m_i = Q, residues a_i such that \bigcup_i (a_i + m_i \mathbb{Z}) covers [1, H] with H > c \frac{\log \log Q \log \log \log \log Q}{(\log \log \log Q)^2} \log Q for any c>0.
moves.rationale: Erd\H{o}s-Rankin method: iteratively construct covering system with moduli primorials or Fermat primes, tuning degrees to optimize H/Q ratio with extra log log factors.
moves.test_plan: Construct explicit moduli sequence (e.g., m_i = next primorial), compute minimal H(Q), verify H/Q exceeds bound asymptotically.
dependencies: existence of arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions of primes for moduli
Chinese Remainder Theorem for pairwise coprime
moves.expected_effect: Proves Milestone 2, enables prime-free intervals via sieve.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Matches subgoal formalization; transfers Euclid-style infinitude indirectly via growing coverings, preconditions certain by prime infinitude.
required_preconditions: pairwise coprime moduli exist with product Q growing
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: For small Q= primorial up to p=20, compute explicit covering density; if H/Q < bound for c=1, reject.

move.claim: \exists infinitely many Q with pairwise coprime m_1, \dots, m_k, \prod m_i = Q, residues a_i such that \bigcup_i (a_i + m_i \mathbb{Z}) covers [1, H] with H > c \frac{\log \log Q \log \log \log \log Q}{(\log \log \log Q)^2} \log Q for any c>0.
move.rationale: Construct via greedy algorithm: select distinct primes p_i ~ \log \log Q \cdot \log \log \log \log Q / \log \log \log Q, set m_i = p_i, a_i chosen to cover uncovered prefix; density analysis yields the bound.
move.test_plan: Implement greedy covering in code for Q up to exp(30), verify H/Q > bound numerically; induct on k for density.
dependencies: Existence of primes in short intervals by sieve methods
Union bound on uncovered proportion <1 - (log Q)/H
move.expected_effect: Proves crt_covering milestone, providing prime-free intervals via sieving.
move.risk: medium, parameter tuning delicate but preconditioned on prime gaps
move.why_this_applies_here: Precisely subgoal 'formalize: [CRT covering]'; transfers Euclid-style product construction to covering systems.
required_preconditions: Primes exist in [y, y + y^{0.5}] for y=exp(log log Q / log log log Q)
move.how_to_falsify_fast: For Q=primorial(20)~exp(50), run greedy cover [1,0.1 log Q * expr]; if uncovered >0, falsify.
